findings.evidence: resolveDisplay mixes three intents in one method: V2 title-case formatting (replaceFirstChar), V1-to-V2 fallback routing (findV2/findV1 branching), and the method's own signature change — as the inline comment on line 29 confirms, these should have been separate steps.
findings.minimal_fix: Split into two commits: first rename-only refactor preserving exact output, then a separate commit adding V2 title-case formatting.
findings.verification: Run display name tests after the rename-only commit and assert identical outputs before proceeding to the behavior commit.

findings.evidence: UserRepository.save() writes to both v1Store and v2Store (lines 14-17), while findV1/findV2 expose both schemas with no migration phase boundary — callers have no contract for when v1Store reads become obsolete.
findings.minimal_fix: Define explicit migration phases: (1) dual-write window with parity tests, (2) V2-read cutover, (3) V1-write removal with a deletion PR checklist.
findings.verification: Add parity tests asserting findV1 and findV2 return equivalent display names for all saved users before entering the cutover phase.

findings.evidence: getDisplayName is @Deprecated with no callers, but remains in the codebase indefinitely.
findings.minimal_fix: Remove getDisplayName after confirming zero call sites via a grep or IDE find-usages check.
findings.verification: Run a full project build and verify no compilation errors or unresolved references after deletion.

findings.evidence: No tests verify display-name parity between V1 fallback and V2 paths during the migration window.
findings.minimal_fix: Add parameterized tests for V2-only, V1-only, and mixed-store scenarios asserting identical display names.
findings.verification: Execute the display name test suite and confirm both code paths produce equivalent outputs for matching user data.
